name: Temporal Chromatic Aberration
description: Chromatic aberration via per-channel temporal frame delay

_h1._role: PERSISTENT history stage 1 (delay 1). Survives frame-to-frame; inits to zero (alpha 0 == empty). Written by shift1 (from inputTex), read by main + shift2.

_h2._role: PERSISTENT history stage 2 (delay 2). Written by shift2 (from _h1), read by main + shift3.

_h3._role: PERSISTENT history stage 3 (delay 3). Written by shift3 (from _h2), read by main + shift4.

_h4._role: PERSISTENT history stage 4 (delay 4). Written by shift4 (from _h3), read by main + shift5.

_h5._role: PERSISTENT history stage 5 (delay 5). Written by shift5 (from _h4), read by main + shift6.

_h6._role: PERSISTENT history stage 6 (delay 6). Written by shift6 (from _h5), read by main + shift7.

_h7._role: PERSISTENT history stage 7 (delay 7). Written by shift7 (from _h6), read by main + shift8.

_h8._role: PERSISTENT history stage 8 (delay 8). Written by shift8 (from _h7), read by main.

feedback._comment: 8-stage bucket-brigade temporal delay line. The 'main' read pass MUST run FIRST (definition.js order), sampling pre-shift history so _h1.._h8 still hold last frame's values. Then eight tail-first shift passes (shift8 first .. shift1 last) advance the line: each stage copies its source's last-frame value BEFORE that source is overwritten this frame (shift1 ingests the live inputTex into _h1). The eight _h1.._h8 textures are PERSISTENT state and must survive frame-to-frame (NOT cleared/reused per frame); they init to zero so alpha==0 marks 'empty', which the read shader falls back to the live frame for ramp-in. No 'repeat:' passes (no _iteration uniform). No MRT/drawBuffers. No blend (all passes Blend Off). Not the canonical 'selfTex' single-frame feedback alias — this is an explicit N-frame delay line wired via the _h* persistent textures.
